text:
COLLEGE STATION, Texas (KBTX) -Texas A&M is looking to make a strong showing at the 2024 Paris Olympics with 24 former and current athletes competing.

Among them is Maggie Malone-Hardin, who is making her third Olympic appearance in the javelin.

Malone-Hardin holds the American record in javelin and has earned multiple All-American honors during her collegiate career.

“I am really grateful just to get to compete one more time and take the experience that I had from the last two and pull it into this third one and overall know that my worth isn’t in the medal that I may or may not receive or on the number that comes up on the screen, but it’s in Jesus at the end of the day,” said Malone-Hardin.

Malone-Hardin says her journey has been marked by perseverance and faith.

“I can’t believe that I’m still a part of this and thinking back to 2016, my very first Olympic Games, and just the person that I was then versus where I’m at now. I have a lot of compassion and a lot of grace for that version of Maggie in 2016,” Malone-Hardin added.

Shaine Casas is making his first Olympic appearance and is known for his versatility in the pool, setting multiple NCAA records.

“I’ve been doing my best to be as prepared and as fit and ready for this moment. And you know if I can kind of give everything I’ve got and execute, I don’t think leaving with a medal is outside of my capabilities,” said Casas.

Casas says while he is excited to be in Paris, he’s focused on living in the moment and enjoying his Olympic experience to the fullest.

“Even if you make it again, it might not be the same thing and the same experience. The first time is probably the most intense and overwhelming and memorable of all. I’m hoping that this first one is awesome. I hope to have more in the future, but my biggest thing is being present,” said Casas

Casas is set to compete in the Men’s 200m Individual Medley on Thursday. The women’s javelin throw qualifying rounds are on August 7.

title: Paris 2024: Texas A&M athletes aim for Olympic glory
description: Texas A&M is looking to make a strong showing at the 2024 Paris Olympics with 24 former and current athletes competing.
